text: The incidence proportion of running-related injury over 12 months in competitive adolescent distance runners is 68%.
The incidence rate of running-related injury in competitive adolescent distance runners is 6.3 per 1,000 participation hours.
The most commonly injured body areas in adolescent distance runners are the knee, foot/toes, and lower leg, primarily caused by overuse.
Training frequency (sessions per week) significantly increases with chronological age in adolescent distance runners.
A large proportion (58%) of competitive adolescent distance runners self-report a high level of sport specialisation.
Overuse is the primary mechanism of running-related injury in competitive adolescent distance runners rather than acute trauma.
